title: Instance Methods
subtitle: Give your objects the ability to do things
learning_objectives: Define instance methods inside a class
Call methods on an object using dot notation
Use self inside methods to access the object's attributes
Write methods that modify attributes and return values

sections.heading: Defining and Calling Instance Methods
sections.content: A method is a function defined inside a class. Like __init__, every method takes self as its first parameter. You call a method using dot notation: object.method().
sections.code_output:
Rex says: Woof!
Rex sits down.

sections.heading: Methods That Take Arguments
sections.content: Methods can take extra arguments just like regular functions. self is always the first parameter, but you can add as many more as you need.
sections.code_output:
Deposited $50. Balance: $150
Withdrew $30. Balance: $120
Insufficient funds!

sections.heading: Methods That Return Values
sections.content: Methods can return values just like regular functions.

exercises.title: Add Methods to a Car
exercises.prompt: Define a Car class with __init__(make, model) and two methods: describe() that prints 'Make: X, Model: Y', and honk() that prints 'Beep beep!'. Create a car and call both methods.

tests.message: Output contains: Make: Honda, Model: Civic

exercises.title: Counter Class
exercises.prompt: Write a Counter class with __init__ (sets self.count = 0), increment() (adds 1), reset() (sets to 0), and get_count() that returns self.count. Create c = Counter(), increment 3 times, print count, reset, print count again.

quiz.question: Why does every instance method have 'self' as its first parameter?
options: It is just a tradition with no meaning
It lets the method access and modify the object's own attributes
Python requires it for syntax but ignores it
quiz.explanation: self is a reference to the specific object the method is being called on. Without self, the method would have no way to access the object's data like self.name or self.balance.

quiz.question: How do you call a method called 'bark' on a Dog object called 'rex'?
quiz.explanation: Use dot notation: rex.bark(). Python automatically passes rex as the 'self' argument, so you don't need to pass anything.

completion_message: Brilliant! Your objects now have behaviour. This is the real power of OOP!
